sample_size.source_quote: A final cohort of 2594 paediatric ICU admission encounters due to infection with known outcomes were identified, with a median age of 13 years (IQR 1–16).

patient_age_mean.source_quote: A final cohort of 2594 paediatric ICU admission encounters due to infection with known outcomes were identified, with a median age of 13 years (IQR 1–16).

icu_mortality_percent.source_quote: 151 (5.8%) children died in hospital and 949 (36.6%) died in hospital or experienced an ICU length of stay of 3 days or more

mortality_predictors.statistical_method: AUROC (area under receiver operating characteristic curve)
mortality_predictors.source_quote: For the primary outcome, discrimination was highest for SOFA (AUROC = 0.829) which was significantly higher than SIRS (AUROC = 0.727, p < 0.001), severe sepsis (AUROC = 0.711, p < 0.001), and qSOFA (AUROC = 0.739, p < 0.001), though not significantly higher than PELOD-2 (AUROC = 0.816, p = 0.970).

The best binary performance for PELOD-2 was using a cutpoint score of ≥ 8, resulting in an adjusted AUROC for in-hospital mortality of 0.812 (95% CI 0.774–0.851), and a sensitivity of 88.1% and a specificity of 55.7%.

mortality_predictors.outcome_definition: Secondary outcome (in-hospital mortality or ICU LOS ≥ 3 days)
mortality_predictors.source_quote: For the secondary outcome, discrimination was highest for PELOD-2 (AUROC = 0.771), which was significantly higher than SIRS (AUROC = 0.676, p < 0.001), severe sepsis (AUROC = 0.677, p < 0.001), qSOFA (AUROC = 0.682, p < 0.001), and SOFA (AUROC = 0.751, p < 0.001)
For the primary outcome, discrimination was highest for SOFA (AUROC = 0.829) which was significantly higher than SIRS (AUROC = 0.727, p < 0.001)

For the primary outcome, discrimination was highest for SOFA (AUROC = 0.829) which was significantly higher than SIRS (AUROC = 0.727, p < 0.001), severe sepsis (AUROC = 0.711, p < 0.001), and qSOFA (AUROC = 0.739, p < 0.001)
For the primary outcome, discrimination was highest for SOFA (AUROC = 0.829) which was significantly higher than SIRS (AUROC = 0.727, p < 0.001), severe sepsis (AUROC = 0.711, p < 0.001)

mortality_predictors.statistical_method: Multivariable mixed effects logistic regression adjusted for baseline risk
mortality_predictors.cutoff_value: ≥2 SIRS criteria, one of which must be abnormal temperature or WCC
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Using models adjusted for baseline risk, the relative odds of death increased in the presence of paediatric SIRS (OR 1.83, 95% CI 1.13–2.99, p = 0.015)

mortality_predictors.statistical_method: Multivariable mixed effects logistic regression adjusted for baseline risk
mortality_predictors.source_quote: the relative odds of death increased in the presence of paediatric SIRS (OR 1.83, 95% CI 1.13–2.99, p = 0.015), or the presence of any ≥ 2 SIRS criteria (OR 2.00, 95% CI 1.10–3.64, p = 0.023).
each additional SIRS criterion was associated with a 50% increase in the relative odds for the primary outcome (OR 1.50, 95% CI 1.25–1.81, p < 0.001)

mortality_predictors.outcome_definition: Secondary outcome (in-hospital mortality or ICU LOS ≥ 3 days)
mortality_predictors.source_quote: For the secondary outcome, discrimination was highest for PELOD-2 (AUROC = 0.771), which was significantly higher than SIRS (AUROC = 0.676, p < 0.001), severe sepsis (AUROC = 0.677, p < 0.001), qSOFA (AUROC = 0.682, p < 0.001), and SOFA (AUROC = 0.751, p < 0.001)
